per_question.expected_preview: Task::ready() creates an immediately-resolved Task<T> without spawning on executor. Found in vim/com
per_question.answer_preview: Task::ready(value) creates already-completed Task. Returns immediately, no async scheduling. Use instead of cx.spawn() when: result already known synchronously, want to return Task for API consistency

per_question.expected_preview: Anyhow's context methods add descriptive information to errors as they propagate. Found throughout Z

per_question.answer_preview: bail!("message") is shorthand for return Err(anyhow!("message")). Difference: bail! includes return, immediately exits function. anyhow!() just creates error, must be wrapped in Err() and returned. Co

per_question.answer_preview: use_key_equivalents enables physical key position matching for non-QWERTY layouts. When true, keymap matches physical key position (QWERTY equivalent) rather than character produced. Example: French A

per_question.answer_preview: Selection invariant: start and end are anchor positions, but NO ordering requirement enforced in raw storage. The Selection stores head (cursor position) and tail (selection anchor). set_head() update

per_question.answer_preview: An edit is considered 'undone' when its undo_count in UndoMap is ODD. undo_count increments with each undo/redo affecting that edit. Even count = visible, odd count = undone. UndoMap.is_undone(edit_id

per_question.expected_preview: Merged operations have their timestamps stored in the merged_operations vector (line 1377 in buffer.
per_question.answer_preview: merge_into_base() for branch buffers moves operations from branch to base buffer. Merged operations: transferred to base's history, removed from branch. Tracking important because: 1) Prevents double-

per_question.answer_preview: Invalid regex patterns cause the permission check to FAIL (deny). If always_allow has invalid regex, it doesn't match anything (commands not allowed). If always_deny has invalid regex, pattern ignored

per_question.answer_preview: was_dirty_before_starting_transaction only tracked at outermost transaction level (depth 0 -> 1 transition). Nested transactions don't re-record dirty state. Constraint: inner transactions inherit out

per_question.answer_preview: SelectionsCollection maintains: disjoint (Vec<Selection>) - committed, non-overlapping selections, pending (Option<Selection>) - in-progress selection being modified. Guarantee: when pending is promot

per_question.answer_preview: check_invariants() (debug-only) verifies fragment IDs are strictly increasing in the fragments SumTree. Invariant: each fragment's locator must be greater than previous. Critical because: fragment ord

per_question.expected_preview: The diff is adjusted to account for intervening edits, and conflicting parts are discarded (line 227
per_question.answer_preview: apply_diff() with stale diff: anchors in diff may not resolve correctly to current buffer positions. Behavior depends on anchor validity: valid anchors apply at best-effort positions, invalid anchors

per_question.answer_preview: ReadOnly capability constraint: edit operations return error or are silently ignored. Buffer.edit() checks capability: if ReadOnly, returns Err or early returns. Prevents: text insertion, deletion, un

per_question.answer_preview: UpdateSelections with older timestamp is ignored/rejected. Each replica's selections tracked with lamport_timestamp. Update only applied if new_timestamp > stored_timestamp. This handles out-of-order

per_question.expected_preview: push_empty_transaction creates a parent transaction without clearing the redo stack (lines 2448-2460
per_question.answer_preview: push_empty_transaction() creates transaction with no edits. Purpose: mark a point in history for grouping, create checkpoint for potential future undo. Caller responsibility: 1) Only call at appropria

per_question.expected_preview: The group_interval field (default 300ms for production, 0 for tests per lines 196-200 in text.rs) de
per_question.answer_preview: History.group_interval (Duration, default 300ms) controls transaction grouping. Invariant: consecutive transactions group only if time between first_edit_at of new and last_edit_at of previous is < gr

per_question.expected_preview: The buffer validates that the anchor's buffer_id matches the snapshot's remote_id (lines 2306-2316 i
per_question.answer_preview: Critical check: anchor.buffer_id must match buffer's remote_id. anchor.is_valid(snapshot) verifies: 1) buffer_id matches or is None, 2) timestamp exists in buffer's history, 3) offset is within valid

per_question.expected_preview: The saved_version is updated in did_save (line 1529) and reload operations (line 1620-1621). When up
per_question.answer_preview: saved_version updated only when buffer successfully saved to disk. Constraint: save operation must complete without error. When saved_version updated: saved_mtime set to file's new mtime, is_dirty imp

per_question.expected_preview: Editor
per_question.answer_preview: The main struct in the editor crate is Editor. It represents a text editor view and contains the buffer, selections, display map, and handles user interactions.

per_question.expected_preview: gpui
per_question.answer_preview: The UI framework crate in Zed is called GPUI (GPU User Interface). It provides the element system, rendering, event handling, and entity management.

per_question.expected_preview: Workspace
per_question.answer_preview: The main struct in the workspace crate is Workspace. It manages the window layout including panes, docks, panels, and coordinates the overall editor interface.

per_question.expected_preview: MultiBuffer
per_question.answer_preview: The struct that represents the multi-buffer is MultiBuffer. It enables displaying multiple buffers or excerpts from the same buffer as a single continuous text stream.

per_question.expected_preview: Project
per_question.answer_preview: The main struct in the project crate is Project. It coordinates buffers, worktrees, language servers, and other project-level functionality.

per_question.expected_preview: Fs
per_question.answer_preview: The trait that represents a filesystem interface is Fs. It abstracts file system operations and has implementations for real and fake/test filesystems.

per_question.expected_preview: Buffer
per_question.answer_preview: The struct used to represent a text buffer is Buffer (in the text crate and buffer crate). It stores text content using a Rope data structure and manages editing operations.

per_question.expected_preview: ThemeRegistry
per_question.answer_preview: The struct for managing themes is ThemeRegistry. It handles loading, caching, and providing access to theme definitions and colors.

per_question.expected_preview: Rope
per_question.answer_preview: The main struct in the rope crate is Rope. It provides an efficient data structure for storing and manipulating large text documents with O(log n) operations.

per_question.expected_preview: LanguageRegistry
per_question.answer_preview: The struct that represents the language registry is LanguageRegistry. It manages language definitions, grammar loading, and language server adapters.

per_question.expected_preview: SettingsStore
per_question.answer_preview: The struct that handles settings is SettingsStore. It manages loading, merging, and providing access to user and project settings.

per_question.expected_preview: BufferDiff
per_question.answer_preview: The main struct for buffer diff operations is BufferDiff. It computes and represents differences between buffer versions for git integration and comparison views.

per_question.expected_preview: main
per_question.answer_preview: The main function is located in crates/zed/src/main.rs. This is the entry point for the Zed application.

per_question.expected_preview: AppState
per_question.answer_preview: AppState in the collab crate holds shared application state for the collaboration server including database connections, configuration, and service handles.

per_question.expected_preview: HighlightId
per_question.answer_preview: The struct that represents an individual highlight ID is HighlightId. It's a newtype wrapper around u32 used to identify syntax highlighting styles.
